attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

27		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models"
28		"github.com/utilitywarehouse/telecom-mobile/services/handset/pkg/protos/generated/v1/apis/handset"
29	)
30	
31	var (
32		requestCounter = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
33			Name: "handset_api_requests_total",
34			Help: "Total number of handset API requests by method and status",
35		}, []string{"method", "status"})
36	
37		callbackCounter = promauto.NewCounterVec(prometheus.CounterOpts{
38			Name: "handset_eligibility_callback_total",
39			Help: "Total number of eligibility callbacks by result",
40		}, []string{"result"})
41	)
42	
43	const callbackSecretHeaderKey = "x-callback-secret" // nolint: gosec
44	
45	//go:generate mockgen -destination=./mocks/$GOFILE -source=./$GOFILE
46	
47	type Service interface {
48		GetActiveContractForService(ctx context.Context, serviceID string) (*models.Contract, error)
49		GetAvailableHandsets(ctx context.Context, customerID, serviceID string, includeOutOfStock bool) ([]models.Handset, error)
50		RequestHandsetForService(ctx context.Context, serviceID, customerID, handsetID string) (string, string, error)
51		EligibilityCallbackSuccess(ctx context.Context, requestID string, outcome models.EligibilityOutcome) error
52		EligibilityCallbackFailure(ctx context.Context, requestID string, outcome models.EligibilityFailureOutcome) error
53		EligibilityStatusUpdate(ctx context.Context, requestID, status string) error
54		GetRequest(ctx context.Context, requestID string) (*models.Contract, *models.Handset, error)
55	}
56	
57	type Controller struct {
58		handset.UnimplementedHandsetAPIServer
59		service        Service
60		callbackSecret string
61	}
62	
63	func NewController(service Service, callbackSecret string) *Controller {
64		return &Controller{
65			service:        service,
66			callbackSecret: callbackSecret,
67		}
68	}
69	
70	func (c *Controller) GetAvailableHandsets(ctx context.Context, request *handset.GetAvailableHandsetsRequest) (*handset.GetAvailableHandsetsResponse, error) {
71		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "GetAvailableHandsets", trace.WithAttributes(
72			attribute.String("service_id", request.GetServiceId()),
73		))
74		var err error
75		defer func() {
76			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
77			span.End()
78			recordRequestMetric("GetAvailableHandsets", err)
79		}()
80	
81		handsets, err := c.service.GetAvailableHandsets(ctx, request.GetCustomerId(), request.GetServiceId(), request.GetIncludeOutOfStock())
82		if err != nil {
83			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "GetAvailableHandsets", err)
84		}
85	
86		var response []*handset.Handset
87		for _, h := range handsets {
88			handsetProto, termErr := handsetToProto(h)
89			if termErr != nil {
90				return nil, status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "failed to parse contract term: %v", termErr)
91			}
92			response = append(response, handsetProto)
93		}
94	
95		return &handset.GetAvailableHandsetsResponse{Handsets: response}, nil
96	}
97	
98	func (c *Controller) GetActiveContractForService(ctx context.Context, request *handset.GetActiveContractForServiceRequest) (*handset.GetActiveContractForServiceResponse, error) {
99		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "GetActiveContractForService", trace.WithAttributes(
100			attribute.String("service_id", request.GetServiceId()),
101		))
102		var err error
103		defer func() {
104			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
105			span.End()
106			recordRequestMetric("GetActiveContractForService", err)
107		}()
108	
109		contract, err := c.service.GetActiveContractForService(ctx, request.GetServiceId())
110		if err != nil {
111			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "GetActiveContractForService", err)
112		}
113	
114		return contractToProto(contract), nil
115	}
116	
117	func (c *Controller) GetRequest(ctx context.Context, request *handset.GetRequestRequest) (*handset.GetRequestResponse, error) {
118		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "GetRequest", trace.WithAttributes(
119			attribute.String("request_id", request.GetRequestId()),
120		))
121		var err error
122		defer func() {
123			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
124			span.End()
125			recordRequestMetric("GetRequest", err)
126		}()
127	
128		// Validate shared secret — this endpoint is for the third-party provider
129		if err = c.validateCallbackSecret(ctx); err != nil {
130			tlog.Warn(ctx, "GetRequest rejected: invalid secret")
131			return nil, err
132		}
133	
134		contract, h, err := c.service.GetRequest(ctx, request.GetRequestId())
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "GetRequest", err)
137		}
138	
139		return &handset.GetRequestResponse{
140			RequestId:        contract.RequestID,
141			CustomerId:       contract.CustomerID,
142			ServiceId:        contract.ServiceID,
143			RequestedProduct: contractToProduct(contract, h),
144		}, nil
145	}
146	
147	func (c *Controller) RequestHandsetForService(ctx context.Context, request *handset.RequestHandsetForServiceRequest) (*handset.RequestHandsetForServiceResponse, error) {
148		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "RequestHandsetForService", trace.WithAttributes(
149			attribute.String("service_id", request.GetServiceId()),
150			attribute.String("handset_id", request.GetHandsetId()),
151			uwconv.CustomerAccountID(request.GetCustomerId()),
152		))
153		var err error
154		defer func() {
155			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
156			span.End()
157			recordRequestMetric("RequestHandsetForService", err)
158		}()
159	
160		requestID, redirectURL, err := c.service.RequestHandsetForService(ctx, request.GetServiceId(), request.GetCustomerId(), request.GetHandsetId())
161		if err != nil {
162			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "RequestHandsetForService", err)
163		}
164	
165		span.SetAttributes(attribute.String("request_id", requestID))
166	
167		return &handset.RequestHandsetForServiceResponse{
168			RequestId:   requestID,
169			HandsetId:   request.GetHandsetId(),
170			Status:      string(models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility),
171			RedirectUrl: redirectURL,
172		}, nil
173	}
174	
175	func (c *Controller) EligibilityCallbackSuccess(ctx context.Context, request *handset.EligibilityCallbackSuccessRequest) (*emptypb.Empty, error) {
176		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "EligibilityCallbackSuccess", trace.WithAttributes(
177			attribute.String("request_id", request.GetRequestId()),
178			attribute.String("decision", request.GetDecision().String()),
179		))
180		var err error
181		defer func() {
182			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
183			span.End()
184			recordRequestMetric("EligibilityCallbackSuccess", err)
185		}()
186	
187		if err = c.validateCallbackSecret(ctx); err != nil {
188			tlog.Warn(ctx, "eligibility callback success rejected: invalid secret")
189			callbackCounter.WithLabelValues("rejected").Inc()
190			return nil, err
191		}
192	
193		outcome := models.EligibilityOutcome{
194			Decision:           request.GetDecision().String(),
195			AgreementID:        request.GetAgreementId(),
196			DocusignEnvelopeID: request.GetDocusignEnvelopeId(),
197			SignedAt:           protoTimestampToTime(request.GetSignedAt()),
198			CompletedAt:        protoTimestampToTime(request.GetCompletedAt()),
199		}
200		err = c.service.EligibilityCallbackSuccess(ctx, request.GetRequestId(), outcome)
201		if err != nil {
202			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "EligibilityCallbackSuccess", err)
203		}
204	
205		callbackCounter.WithLabelValues("success").Inc()
206		return &emptypb.Empty{}, nil
207	}
208	
209	func (c *Controller) EligibilityCallbackFailure(ctx context.Context, request *handset.EligibilityCallbackFailureRequest) (*emptypb.Empty, error) {
210		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "EligibilityCallbackFailure", trace.WithAttributes(
211			attribute.String("request_id", request.GetRequestId()),
212			attribute.String("failure_reason", request.GetFailureReason().String()),
213		))
214		var err error
215		defer func() {
216			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
217			span.End()
218			recordRequestMetric("EligibilityCallbackFailure", err)
219		}()
220	
221		if err = c.validateCallbackSecret(ctx); err != nil {
222			tlog.Warn(ctx, "eligibility callback failure rejected: invalid secret")
223			callbackCounter.WithLabelValues("rejected").Inc()
224			return nil, err
225		}
226	
227		failureOutcome := models.EligibilityFailureOutcome{
228			FailureReason:  request.GetFailureReason().String(),
229			FailureStage:   request.GetFailureStage().String(),
230			FailureMessage: request.GetFailureMessage(),
231			FailedAt:       protoTimestampToTime(request.GetFailedAt()),
232		}
233		err = c.service.EligibilityCallbackFailure(ctx, request.GetRequestId(), failureOutcome)
234		if err != nil {
235			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "EligibilityCallbackFailure", err)
236		}
237	
238		callbackCounter.WithLabelValues("failure").Inc()
239		return &emptypb.Empty{}, nil
240	}
241	
242	func (c *Controller) EligibilityStatusUpdate(ctx context.Context, request *handset.EligibilityStatusUpdateRequest) (*emptypb.Empty, error) {
243		ctx, span := tracing.Tracer().Start(ctx, "EligibilityStatusUpdate", trace.WithAttributes(
244			attribute.String("request_id", request.GetRequestId()),
245			attribute.String("status", request.GetStatus().String()),
246		))
247		var err error
248		defer func() {
249			_ = tracing.RecordSpanError(span, err)
250			span.End()
251			recordRequestMetric("EligibilityStatusUpdate", err)
252		}()
253	
254		if err = c.validateCallbackSecret(ctx); err != nil {
255			tlog.Warn(ctx, "eligibility callback status update rejected: invalid secret")
256			callbackCounter.WithLabelValues("rejected").Inc()
257			return nil, err
258		}
259	
260		err = c.service.EligibilityStatusUpdate(ctx, request.GetRequestId(), request.GetStatus().String())
261		if err != nil {
262			return nil, toGRPCError(ctx, "EligibilityStatusUpdate", err)
263		}
264	
265		return &emptypb.Empty{}, nil
266	}
267	
268	func (c *Controller) validateCallbackSecret(ctx context.Context) error {
269		if c.callbackSecret == "" {
270			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "callback secret is not configured")
271		}
272	
273		md, ok := metadata.FromIncomingContext(ctx)
274		if !ok {
275			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "missing metadata")
276		}
277	
278		values := md.Get(callbackSecretHeaderKey)
279		if len(values) != 1 || !hmac.Equal([]byte(values[0]), []byte(c.callbackSecret)) {
280			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "invalid callback secret")
281		}
282	
283		return nil
284	}
285	
286	func toGRPCError(ctx context.Context, method string, err error) error {
287		switch {
288		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrNotFound):
289			return status.Error(codes.NotFound, err.Error())
290		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrBadRequest):
291			return status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error())
292		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrFailedValidation):
293			return status.Error(codes.FailedPrecondition, err.Error())
294		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrUnauthenticated):
295			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
296		default:
297			tlog.Error(ctx, "%s failed: %v", method, err)
298			return status.Error(codes.Internal, "internal server error")
299		}
300	}
301	
302	func contractToProto(contract *models.Contract) *handset.GetActiveContractForServiceResponse {
303		return &handset.GetActiveContractForServiceResponse{
304			Contract: &handset.Contract{
305				Id:        contract.ID,
306				CreatedAt: contract.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"),
307				Status:    string(contract.Status),
308			},
309		}
310	}
311	
312	func contractToProduct(contract *models.Contract, h *models.Handset) *handset.ContractProduct {
313		name := contract.HandsetName
314		basePricePence := parsePenceOrZero(contract.RetailPricePence)
315		monthlyRepaymentPence := parsePenceOrZero(contract.ContractPricePence)
316		contractMonths := parsePenceOrZero(contract.ContractMonths)
317	
318		if h != nil {
319			if name == "" {
320				name = h.CurrentProperties.Name
321			}
322			if basePricePence == 0 {
323				basePricePence = h.CurrentProperties.RRPPence
324			}
325			if monthlyRepaymentPence == 0 {
326				monthlyRepaymentPence = h.CurrentProperties.RentalPricePence
327			}
328			if contractMonths == 0 {
329				contractMonths = h.CurrentProperties.ContractTermMonths
330			}
331		}
332	
333		// Avoid int->int32 wraparound on unexpected/corrupted data.
334		basePrice32 := int32(0)
335		if basePricePence >= math.MinInt32 && basePricePence <= math.MaxInt32 {
336			basePrice32 = int32(basePricePence) // nolint: gosec
337		}
338		monthlyRepayment32 := int32(0)
339		if monthlyRepaymentPence >= math.MinInt32 && monthlyRepaymentPence <= math.MaxInt32 {
340			monthlyRepayment32 = int32(monthlyRepaymentPence) // nolint: gosec
341		}
342	
343		// @todo hardcoded APR for now, this is all for testing purposes and should be replaced with real calculations based on the contract and handset details
344		aprPercent := 5.0
345		interest := totalInterest(basePricePence, aprPercent/100, contractMonths)
346		totalFinance := int32(monthlyRepaymentPence*contractMonths) + interest
347	
348		return &handset.ContractProduct{
349			Id:               contract.HandsetID,
350			Name:             name,
351			BasePrice:        basePrice32,
352			MonthlyRepayment: monthlyRepayment32,
353			TotalFinance:     totalFinance,
354			InterestTotal:    interest,
355			AprPercent:       float32(aprPercent),
356		}
357	}
358	
359	func totalInterest(principalPence int, apr float64, months int) int32 {
360		principal := float64(principalPence)
361	
362		r := apr / 12.0 // e.g. 12% APR => 0.12
363	
364		payment := principal *
365			(r * math.Pow(1+r, float64(months))) /
366			(math.Pow(1+r, float64(months)) - 1)
367	
368		interest := payment*float64(months) - principal
369	
370		return int32(math.Round(interest))
371	}
372	
373	func parsePenceOrZero(s string) int {
374		if s == "" {
375			return 0
376		}
377		v, err := strconv.Atoi(s)
378		if err != nil {
379			return 0
380		}
381		return v
382	}
383	
384	func recordRequestMetric(method string, err error) {
385		if err != nil {
386			requestCounter.WithLabelValues(method, "error").Inc()
387		} else {
388			requestCounter.WithLabelValues(method, "ok").Inc()
389		}
390	}
391	
392	func toContractTerm(h models.HandsetProperties) (int32, error) {
393		i := h.ContractTermMonths
394		if i < math.MinInt32 || i > math.MaxInt32 {
395			return 0, fmt.Errorf("contract term value %d overflows int32", i)
396		}
397		return int32(i), nil // nolint: gosec
398	}
399	
400	func handsetToProto(h models.Handset) (*handset.Handset, error) {
401		term, err := toContractTerm(h.CurrentProperties)
402		if err != nil {
403			return nil, err
404		}
405	
406		p := h.CurrentProperties
407		return &handset.Handset{
408			Id:                 h.ID,
409			Name:               p.Name,
410			RentalPrice:        toMoney(p.RentalPricePence),
411			Rrp:                toMoney(p.RRPPence),
412			ContractTermMonths: term,
413			Manufacturer:       p.Manufacturer,
414			Model:              p.Model,
415			Colours:            p.Colours,
416			Capacity:           p.Capacity,
417			ScreenSize:         p.ScreenSize,
418			Battery:            p.Battery,
419			SdCapacity:         p.SDCapacity,
420			WifiStandard:       p.WiFiStandard,
421			Esim:               p.ESim,
422			DualSim:            p.DualSim,
423			SaleStatus:         p.SaleStatus,
424			IsNew:              p.IsNew,
425			SpecificationsLink: p.SpecificationsLink,
426			InStock:            h.StockBase-h.OrdersPlaced > 0,
427		}, nil
428	}
429	
430	func protoTimestampToTime(ts *timestamppb.Timestamp) *time.Time {
431		if ts == nil {
432			return nil
433		}
434		t := ts.AsTime()
435		return &t
436	}
437	
438	func toMoney(pence int) *serviceevents.Money {
439		pounds := float64(pence) / 100.0
440		return &serviceevents.Money{
441			Amount:   fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", pounds),
442			Currency: "GBP",
443		}
444	}
445

content.text: Now I have enough to give concrete findings. Let me also check the `auth` package to confirm the `AccountID` vs `AccountNumber` field names.

Now I have the full picture. Here are the findings:

---

**1. `lowestPrice` treats price=0 as "no minimum seen yet" — a legitimately free handset is silently skipped**

File: `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/helpers.go`, line 88

```go
if minPence == 0 || pence < minPence {
```

`minPence` is initialised to `0` and used as a sentinel to mean "not yet set". If any handset genuinely has `RentalPrice.Amount = "0.00"` or `"0"`, `parsePenceFromMoney` returns `0`, the condition is true, `minPence` stays `0`, and the currency is updated. If a *second* handset then has `pence = 0` too, `0 < 0` is false so its currency is not picked — still technically "correct" — but the core problem is that a free handset is indistinguishable from "sentinel not set". Worse: if the empty-string case (below) were ever fixed to return 0 instead of an error, this sentinel logic would treat it as a valid zero-price and output `"0.00"` as the from-price rather than skipping.

**Failure scenario:** Any brand that offers a free trial handset (price `"0.00"`) gets its from-price resolved correctly *only* by coincidence of iteration order; the currency could end up from a different handset if the free one is processed first but a later handset also has pence=0.

---

**2. `parsePenceFromMoney("")` returns an error, causing the whole resolver to 500 on any handset with a nil/missing rental price**

File: `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/helpers.go`, lines 13–19 and 81–83

`strconv.ParseFloat("", 64)` returns an error. The grouping loop in the resolver (schema.resolvers.go line 1017) only skips handsets with empty `manufacturer` or `model` — it does not skip handsets with an empty `RentalPrice`. If the handset service returns a handset that has no rental price set (proto field absent, `GetAmount()` returns `""`), `lowestPrice` errors and the entire `MobileAvailableHandsets` resolver returns a 500 to the client.

**Failure scenario:** One handset record in the DB has a missing/null rental price. Every call to `MobileAvailableHandsets` for a service that could be offered that handset hard-errors.

---

**3. The test sets `AccountNumber: accountNo` but the resolver passes `customerAuth.AccountID` as `CustomerId` to the handset gRPC call**

File: `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/schema.resolvers_test.go`, line 1829; `schema.resolvers.go`, line 1006

The test mock returns `&auth.AuthCustomerResponse{AccountNumber: accountNo}` with no `AccountID` set (so `AccountID == ""`). The resolver then calls:

```go
CustomerId: customerAuth.AccountID,
```

The mock for `GetAvailableHandsets` uses `gomock.Any()` so it accepts the empty string without complaint. The test passes, but it silently verifies that `CustomerId` is always forwarded as `""`. In production the real authorizer populates `AccountID`, so the behaviour is correct — but the test gives no protection: if someone changed the resolver to use `AccountNumber` here instead (which would be wrong because `AccountID` is the UUID), the test would still pass.

**Failure scenario:** Not a runtime bug today, but the test provides zero regression coverage on which field is forwarded as `CustomerId`. A refactor that swaps `AccountID` for `AccountNumber` (or vice-versa) would go undetected.

---

**4. `lowestPrice` can return `{Amount: "0.00", Currency: ""}` when the input slice is empty**

File: `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/helpers.go`, lines 77–97

If `hs` is empty (which cannot happen today because `buildPhone` is only called after grouping, but the grouping could in theory produce an empty group through a future code path), the loop body never executes, `minPence` stays 0, `currency` stays `""`, and the function returns a non-nil `MobileMoney` with amount `"0.00"` and no currency rather than an error or nil.

**Failure scenario:** Future caller passes an empty slice; the GraphQL response includes a `fromPrice` of `"0.00"` with no currency rather than a null or an error.

---

**5. `InStock` in `controller.go` uses bare integer subtraction on potentially-negative inputs**

File: `services/handset/internal/grpc/controller.go`, line 426

```go
InStock: h.StockBase-h.OrdersPlaced > 0,
```

Both fields are `int` (from the `models.Handset` struct). If `OrdersPlaced` ever exceeds `StockBase` (a race condition the service attempts to prevent but does not make impossible for negative stock base values or data corruption), `StockBase - OrdersPlaced` is negative, which is `> 0` == false — so that's actually fine directionally. But if `StockBase` is 0 and `OrdersPlaced` is 0 (default values for a newly upserted handset with no stock configured), the result is `0 > 0 == false`, meaning it's marked out of stock, which is correct. The real risk is the signed arithmetic: nothing prevents `StockBase` from being stored as a negative number, at which point `StockBase - OrdersPlaced` could overflow or produce a misleading result. No range/validity check exists before this line.

**Failure scenario:** A sheet-parse bug (the codebase has had several, see recent commits) writes a negative `StockBase`. The subtraction overflows or yields a counter-intuitive `InStock` value, and handsets become incorrectly listed as in-stock.

---

**6. `buildPhone` panics on an empty slice (`hs[0]` with no bounds check)**

File: `services/public-graphql/internal/graph/helpers.go`, line 34

```go
first := hs[0]
```

`buildPhones` calls `buildPhone(modelName, modelMap[modelName])` for every key in the model map. The model map is populated by appending into a nil slice (`grouped[manufacturer][model] = append(..., h)`), so every group has at least one element — *today*. But `buildPhone` itself has no guard, so any future refactor that can produce an empty slice (e.g. filtering by stock inside the grouping loop after the map is already built) will panic at runtime.

**Failure scenario:** A future change filters the handset slice to in-stock-only *after* it has been placed in the group map, leaving an empty slice. `buildPhone` panics with an index-out-of-range, crashing the goroutine/request.
